//@version=6
indicator('Mean Reversion Threshold Levels', overlay = true)


// Input for multiple symbols with their respective threshold levels
input_text = input.text_area('', title = 'Input Symbol Thresholds (Format: SYMBOL on one line, thresholds on next line)', confirm = true)


// Get current symbol
current_symbol = syminfo.ticker


// Color inputs for different levels
color0 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 1')
color1 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 2')
color2 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 3')
color3 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 4')
color4 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 5')
color5 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 6')
color6 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 7')
color7 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 8')
color8 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 9')
color9 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 10')


// Function to split string by a delimiter
TxtToArray(string origin, string divider) =>
str.split(origin, divider)


// Parse the input text to get symbol-specific thresholds
ParseSymbolThresholds(string inputText) =>
symbolThresholds = array.new_string()
if str.length(inputText) > 0
lines = TxtToArray(inputText, '\n')
for i = 0 to array.size(lines) - 1 by 1
line = array.get(lines, i)
// If this is a symbol line (not containing commas)
if not str.contains(line, ',') and str.length(line) > 0
// Check if next line exists and contains thresholds
if i < array.size(lines) - 1
nextLine = array.get(lines, i + 1)
if str.contains(nextLine, ',')
// Add the symbol and its thresholds to our array
array.push(symbolThresholds, line)
array.push(symbolThresholds, nextLine)
symbolThresholds


// Get thresholds for the current symbol
GetCurrentSymbolThresholds(array<string> symbolThresholds, string currentSymbol) =>
result = ''
for i = 0 to array.size(symbolThresholds) - 1 by 2
if i + 1 < array.size(symbolThresholds)
symbol = array.get(symbolThresholds, i)
thresholds = array.get(symbolThresholds, i + 1)


// Handle futures symbol conversion: if input has "=F" ending, check against "1!" TradingView format
symbol_modified = symbol
if str.endswith(symbol, '=F')
symbol_modified := str.replace(symbol, '=F', '1!')
symbol_modified


if symbol == currentSymbol or symbol_modified == currentSymbol
result := thresholds
break
result


// Parse the input to get symbol-specific thresholds
symbolThresholds = ParseSymbolThresholds(input_text)


// Get thresholds for the current symbol
currentThresholds = GetCurrentSymbolThresholds(symbolThresholds, current_symbol)


// Split the current thresholds string into an array
Dividedstrings = TxtToArray(currentThresholds, ',')


// Define a temporary color variable for labels
color temporarycolor = na


// Plot the threshold levels
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 2 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 1)) : na, title = 'level 1', color = color0, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 4 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 3)) : na, title = 'level 2', color = color1, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 6 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 5)) : na, title = 'level 3', color = color2, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 8 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 7)) : na, title = 'level 4', color = color3, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 10 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 9)) : na, title = 'level 5', color = color4, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 12 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 11)) : na, title = 'level 6', color = color5, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 14 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 13)) : na, title = 'level 7', color = color6, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 16 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 15)) : na, title = 'level 8', color = color7, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 18 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 17)) : na, title = 'level 9', color = color8, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 20 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 19)) : na, title = 'level 10', color = color9, display = display.price_scale)


// Add labels and lines only on the last bar
if barstate.islastconfirmedhistory and array.size(Dividedstrings) != 0
for i = 0 to array.size(Dividedstrings) - 1 by 1
if i % 2 == 0
// Determine color based on the current level
if i == 0 or i == 1
temporarycolor := color0
temporarycolor
if i == 2 or i == 3
temporarycolor := color1
temporarycolor
if i == 4 or i == 5
temporarycolor := color2
temporarycolor
if i == 6 or i == 7
temporarycolor := color3
temporarycolor
if i == 8 or i == 9
temporarycolor := color4
temporarycolor
if i == 10 or i == 11
temporarycolor := color5
temporarycolor
if i == 12 or i == 13
temporarycolor := color6
temporarycolor
if i == 14 or i == 15
temporarycolor := color7
temporarycolor
if i == 16 or i == 17
temporarycolor := color8
temporarycolor
if i == 18 or i == 19
temporarycolor := color9
temporarycolor


// Add labels for even indices
if i % 2 == 0
if array.size(Dividedstrings) > i + 1
label.new(bar_index, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i + 1)), array.get(Dividedstrings, i), xloc = xloc.bar_index, color = color.new(color.white, 10), size = size.large, style = label.style_none, textcolor = temporarycolor, textalign = text.align_right)


// Add lines for odd indices
if i % 2 != 0
if array.size(Dividedstrings) > i
line.new(bar_index, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i)), bar_index + 1, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i)), extend = extend.both, color = temporarycolor)

//@version=6
indicator('Mean Reversion Threshold Levels', overlay = true)


// Input for multiple symbols with their respective threshold levels
input_text = input.text_area('', title = 'Input Symbol Thresholds (Format: SYMBOL on one line, thresholds on next line)', confirm = true)


// Get current symbol
current_symbol = syminfo.ticker


// Color inputs for different levels
color0 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 1')
color1 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 2')
color2 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 3')
color3 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 4')
color4 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 5')
color5 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 6')
color6 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 7')
color7 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 8')
color8 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 9')
color9 = input.color(defval = color.rgb(224, 10, 110), title = 'Color 10')


// Function to split string by a delimiter
TxtToArray(string origin, string divider) =>
str.split(origin, divider)


// Parse the input text to get symbol-specific thresholds
ParseSymbolThresholds(string inputText) =>
symbolThresholds = array.new_string()
if str.length(inputText) > 0
lines = TxtToArray(inputText, '\n')
for i = 0 to array.size(lines) - 1 by 1
line = array.get(lines, i)
// If this is a symbol line (not containing commas)
if not str.contains(line, ',') and str.length(line) > 0
// Check if next line exists and contains thresholds
if i < array.size(lines) - 1
nextLine = array.get(lines, i + 1)
if str.contains(nextLine, ',')
// Add the symbol and its thresholds to our array
array.push(symbolThresholds, line)
array.push(symbolThresholds, nextLine)
symbolThresholds


// Get thresholds for the current symbol
GetCurrentSymbolThresholds(array<string> symbolThresholds, string currentSymbol) =>
result = ''
for i = 0 to array.size(symbolThresholds) - 1 by 2
if i + 1 < array.size(symbolThresholds)
symbol = array.get(symbolThresholds, i)
thresholds = array.get(symbolThresholds, i + 1)


// Handle futures symbol conversion: if input has "=F" ending, check against "1!" TradingView format
symbol_modified = symbol
if str.endswith(symbol, '=F')
symbol_modified := str.replace(symbol, '=F', '1!')
symbol_modified


if symbol == currentSymbol or symbol_modified == currentSymbol
result := thresholds
break
result


// Parse the input to get symbol-specific thresholds
symbolThresholds = ParseSymbolThresholds(input_text)


// Get thresholds for the current symbol
currentThresholds = GetCurrentSymbolThresholds(symbolThresholds, current_symbol)


// Split the current thresholds string into an array
Dividedstrings = TxtToArray(currentThresholds, ',')


// Define a temporary color variable for labels
color temporarycolor = na


// Plot the threshold levels
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 2 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 1)) : na, title = 'level 1', color = color0, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 4 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 3)) : na, title = 'level 2', color = color1, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 6 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 5)) : na, title = 'level 3', color = color2, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 8 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 7)) : na, title = 'level 4', color = color3, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 10 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 9)) : na, title = 'level 5', color = color4, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 12 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 11)) : na, title = 'level 6', color = color5, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 14 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 13)) : na, title = 'level 7', color = color6, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 16 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 15)) : na, title = 'level 8', color = color7, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 18 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 17)) : na, title = 'level 9', color = color8, display = display.price_scale)
plot(array.size(Dividedstrings) >= 20 ? str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, 19)) : na, title = 'level 10', color = color9, display = display.price_scale)


// Add labels and lines only on the last bar
if barstate.islastconfirmedhistory and array.size(Dividedstrings) != 0
for i = 0 to array.size(Dividedstrings) - 1 by 1
if i % 2 == 0
// Determine color based on the current level
if i == 0 or i == 1
temporarycolor := color0
temporarycolor
if i == 2 or i == 3
temporarycolor := color1
temporarycolor
if i == 4 or i == 5
temporarycolor := color2
temporarycolor
if i == 6 or i == 7
temporarycolor := color3
temporarycolor
if i == 8 or i == 9
temporarycolor := color4
temporarycolor
if i == 10 or i == 11
temporarycolor := color5
temporarycolor
if i == 12 or i == 13
temporarycolor := color6
temporarycolor
if i == 14 or i == 15
temporarycolor := color7
temporarycolor
if i == 16 or i == 17
temporarycolor := color8
temporarycolor
if i == 18 or i == 19
temporarycolor := color9
temporarycolor


// Add labels for even indices
if i % 2 == 0
if array.size(Dividedstrings) > i + 1
label.new(bar_index, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i + 1)), array.get(Dividedstrings, i), xloc = xloc.bar_index, color = color.new(color.white, 10), size = size.large, style = label.style_none, textcolor = temporarycolor, textalign = text.align_right)


// Add lines for odd indices
if i % 2 != 0
if array.size(Dividedstrings) > i
line.new(bar_index, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i)), bar_index + 1, str.tonumber(array.get(Dividedstrings, i)), extend = extend.both, color = temporarycolor)

Alright, Sharcus, lemme school you on how trading actually works, kiddo. You buy ANY amount of shares at a given price. This could be one, could be eight, whatever. If the price moves x %, your entire position moves x %. So if you bought at $1 and it shoots up to $2, that’s a 100% gain. Eight shares? You made $8. Two shares? You only made $2. Simple math.
Now, if tomorrow i'd say i’m risking 'half of yesterday’s profit' just as alma did, where i set my stop-loss depends on how many shares i had yesterday. Made $8? I risk $4. Made $2? I risk $1. Capiche? We’re risking profit, not the entry price.

comment.body:
Alright, Sharcus, lemme school you on how trading actually works, kiddo. You buy ANY amount of shares at a given price. This could be one, could be eight, whatever. If the price moves x %, your entire position moves x %. So if you bought at $1 and it shoots up to $2, that’s a 100% gain. Eight shares? You made $8. Two shares? You only made $2. Simple math.
Now, if tomorrow i'd say i’m risking 'half of yesterday’s profit' just as alma did, where i set my stop-loss depends on how many shares i had yesterday. Made $8? I risk $4. Made $2? I risk $1. Capiche? We’re risking profit, not the entry price.

when we subscribed, there was an unspoken agreement (sometimes spoken as well) that a minimum level of personal responsibility was expected. Your trades are your own, your entries are your own, your exits are your own, etc. Her callouts have been great, market anomalies aside, but even then you should only be using her callouts for entry/exit as extra confluence to your own trading plan. comment.body: Hey friends; my take is that the tariff negotiations will not be as smooth as many are hoping for. The main problem, as I see it, is that the admin has either sidelined or fired most of the regional experts because they didn’t see the bottom line value of such skills upon assuming office. Blame DOGE for this. This is turning into a tremendous problem because it’s hard to negotiate complex trade deals without have a grasp of the market and foreign policy idiosyncrasies. We are seeing this a bit with Japan right now and it will become more pronounced with other non-allied countries and probably more so, with Russia. There are talks this weekend but I don’t expect them to yield many results since the US side has neglected to utilize any of the traditional foreign policy assets that would allow them to have some sort of leverage in negotiations. Instead, many of these policy makers are going into meeting with little, if any briefings, that give context, that would allow rhetoric US to have any semblance of an upper hand in negotiations. This is the result of cutting things without a deep understanding of what you are actually cutting.
